file_header:
<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<sch:schema xmlns:sch="http://purl.oclc.org/dsdl/schematron" queryBinding="xslt2">
  <sch:ns prefix="xsi" uri="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>
  <sch:ns prefix="rdf" uri="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"/>
  <sch:ns prefix="xlink" uri="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"/>
  <sch:ns prefix="dc" uri="http://www.purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"/>

conversion_rules.prohibited_flag0: XPath from objectPath goes in sch:rule @context. test is always false(). If the context matches, the rule always fails.
conversion_rules.mandatory_no_values_flag1: Parent element goes in @context. test verifies the child exists.
conversion_rules.allowed_values: Element or attribute goes in @context. test is an exists() OR chain: exists(node[(string(.) = 'val1') or (string(.) = 'val2')])
conversion_rules.no_flag_with_values: Same as allowed_values but context is the element containing the values.
conversion_rules.assert_message: Use the objectUse text from the BREX rule as the assert message content.
